---
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---
# Unified Style for Statistical Charts and Figures

Main Content:

1. Methods for generating article figures.

- For those drawn using R code, the code should be provided. Input, output, dependencies, and runtime environment should be noted.
- For those further processed after drawing with R, the processing steps should be explained.
- For those drawn using other methods, the generation method should be clearly marked in this document.

2. Source data corresponding to all figures and tables.

Output Description:

1. publish: Images generated by code.
2. data: Commonly used data for plotting, which may require further processing.
3. source data: Source data, the data directly used for plotting.
4. figure_process: Processing images using software such as Adobe Illustrator.

# Correspondence Table Between Main Figure Numbers and Image Tags

After updating the figure layout, main figure numbers will change. Updating this file accordingly is a bit troublesome. Solution: Do not use figure numbers for naming in figure_process.r; use tags instead. Add a mapping table at the bottom of figure_process.r for the correspondence between main figure numbers and image tags.

```{r}

figID_to_codeID <- c(
  "fig4Ext.e" = "## Fig. 4 Ext. f Micro genes in ST", 
  "fig4Ext.f" = "## Fig. 4 Ext. e：PIGs (PL vs AD)", 
  ""
)

```


# Load required functions for plotting

```{r}
# Plotting
library(ggplot2)

# Data processing
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(reshape2)

# Image adjustment
library(ggtext) # Use markdown to control axis title style (element_markdown).
library(ggforce) # Precisely control point size when drawing spatial plots.
library(patchwork) # Patchwork (wrap_plots)
library(ggpubr) # Add statistical significance annotations (stat_compare_means); R for correlation analysis, significance annotations (stat_cor)

group.fill <- c(
  "WT-CTRL" = "#FBDCBF",
  "5xFAD-CTRL" = "#F39B9A",
  "5xFAD-PLX" = "#BBBBDE"
)
group.stroke <- c(
  "WT-CTRL" = "#D8AC63", 
  "5xFAD-CTRL" = "#E87974",
  "5xFAD-PLX" = "#9E96C9"
)

# load utils ----
# 12 pt font size is a bit too large, 10 pt might be better.
# New version setting: subtract 2 uniformly
# title: 10 pt + Bold
# facet title: 10 + Bold
# axis title: 9 + Bold
# axis text: 8

# Old version setting:
# plot title: 12 pt + Bold
# facet title: 12 pt
# axis title: 11 pt + Bold
# axis text: 10 pt + Bold

# Axis general
axis_linewidth <- 1 * 0.35278 # 1 pt
standard_textsize <- 9 # pt

# Graphics general
geom_linewidth <- 1.5 * 0.35278 # 1.5 pt
errorbar_width <- 2 # Width
errorbar_linewidth <- axis_linewidth
jitter_width <- 0.5

# Significance annotation general
signif_tip_length = 0
signif_colour = "black"
signif_textsize = standard_textsize * 0.35278
signif_linewidth = axis_linewidth

theme_nature <- function() {
  theme_classic() +
    theme(
      text = element_text(family = "Arial", color = "black"),
      axis.line = element_line(linewidth = axis_linewidth), # 0.5 pt equals 0.176388889 mm
      axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = axis_linewidth),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"), 
      strip.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"), 
      strip.background = element_blank(), strip.clip = "off", 
      plot.title = element_text(
        size = 10,
        hjust = 0.5,
        face = "bold"
      ),
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm")
    )
}

# barplot: 
# Data analysis: fig4d-e, fig5a, fig5c, fig5g, fig7a, fig7b
# Experiment: fig5b, fig6e, fig8h

#' Create geometric object layers for bar plots
#'
#' @param bar_width Bar width, default is 0.6
#' @param bar_size Bar border size, default is 0.5
#' @param errorbar_width Error bar width, default is 0.2
#' @param point_size Point size, default is 1.5
#' @param point_shape Point shape, default is 21 (filled circle)
#' @param point_stroke Point border size, default is 0.5
#' @param jitter_width Jitter width, default is 0.2
#' @return List of geometric object layers
geom_custom_barplot <- function(bar_linewidth = NULL, 
                                errorbar_linewidth = NULL, 
                                point_stroke = NULL, 
                                point_fill="white") {
  if(is.null(bar_linewidth)) {
    bar_linewidth <- geom_linewidth # Use external variable if not provided
  }
  if(is.null(errorbar_linewidth)) {
    errorbar_linewidth <- axis_linewidth # Use external variable if not provided
  }
  if(is.null(point_stroke)) {
    point_stroke <- geom_linewidth # Use external variable if not provided
  }
  list(
    geom_bar(
      stat = "summary",
      fun = mean,
      linewidth = geom_linewidth,
      width = 0.6
    ),
    geom_errorbar(
      stat = "summary",
      fun.data = mean_se,
      width = 0.2, 
      linewidth = errorbar_linewidth
    ),
    geom_point(
      position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.5),
      size = 1.5,
      shape = 21,
      stroke = point_stroke, 
      fill="white"
    ), 
    scale_fill_manual(values = group.fill), 
    scale_color_manual(values = group.stroke), 
    theme_nature(), 
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
        legend.position = "none", 
        axis.title.x = element_blank(), 
        axis.title.y = element_markdown())
  )
}

# barplot: 
# Data analysis: fig4d-e, fig5a, fig5c, fig5g, fig7a, fig7b
# Experiment: fig5b, fig6e, fig8h

#' Create geometric object layers for bar plots with facets
#'
#' @param bar_width Bar width, default is 0.6
#' @param bar_size Bar border size, default is 0.5
#' @param errorbar_width Error bar width, default is 0.2
#' @param point_size Point size, default is 1.5
#' @param point_shape Point shape, default is 21 (filled circle)
#' @param point_stroke Point border size, default is 0.5
#' @param jitter_width Jitter width, default is 0.2
#' @return List of geometric object layers
geom_custom_barplot.facet <- function(bar_linewidth = NULL, 
                                errorbar_linewidth = NULL, 
                                point_stroke = NULL, 
                                point_fill="white") {
  if(is.null(bar_linewidth)) {
    bar_linewidth <- geom_linewidth # Use external variable if not provided
  }
  if(is.null(errorbar_linewidth)) {
    errorbar_linewidth <- axis_linewidth # Use external variable if not provided
  }
  if(is.null(point_stroke)) {
    point_stroke <- geom_linewidth # Use external variable if not provided
  }
  list(
    geom_bar(
      stat = "summary",
      fun = mean,
      linewidth = geom_linewidth,
      width = 0.6
    ),
    geom_errorbar(
      stat = "summary",
      fun.data = mean_se,
      width = 0.2, 
      linewidth = errorbar_linewidth
    ),
    geom_jitter(
      size = 1.5,
      shape = 21,
      stroke = point_stroke, 
      fill="white"
    ), 
    scale_fill_manual(values = group.fill), 
    scale_color_manual(values = group.stroke), 
    theme_nature(), 
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
        legend.position = "none", 
        axis.title.x = element_blank(), 
        axis.title.y = element_markdown())
  )
}

# Correlation plot: fig4Extd, fig5e, fig8b
# Point size:


# spatialplot: fig4c_right, fig5d, fig5f (this one is special), fig7d, fig7e

# Ignore boxplot (fig7f-g), heatmap, and UMAP plot for now.

getSTGeneExpression <- function(gene_symbol) {
  gene.url <- paste0("https://f005.backblazeb2.com/file/plx5622/ST/",
                     gene_symbol,
                     ".rds")
  readRDS(url(gene.url))
}

getSTGeneListExpression <- function(gene_list) {
  gene_list.expr <- lapply(gene_list, getSTGeneExpression)
  m <- do.call(rbind, gene_list.expr)
  row.names(m) <- gene_list
  m
}

getSNGeneExpression <- function(gene_symbol) {
  gene.url <- paste0("https://f005.backblazeb2.com/file/plx5622/SN/",
                     gene_symbol,
                     ".rds")
  readRDS(url(gene.url))
}

ggsave.plus <- function(p.tag, p, width, height) {
  write.csv(p@data, paste0("source_data/", p.tag, ".csv"))
  ggsave(paste0("publish_new/", p.tag, ".png"), p, width=width, height=height, dpi=300)
  ggsave(paste0("publish_new/", p.tag, ".pdf"), p, width=width, height=height, device=cairo_pdf)
}

```

# Preprocessing frequently called data

```{r}
# env: dhlab
# workdir: /home/mouse_ad/plot_gene/shinyapp

# ST------------
library(Giotto)
setwd("/home/mouse_ad/plot_gene/shinyapp")
gobj <- readRDS("../../001ST/001Giotto_out/testcombo.rds")
dir.create("data/ST.each_gene")
gobj.expr <- get_expression_values(gobj, values="normalized", output="matrix")

## Prepare metadata ST ----
meta.st <- pDataDT(gobj)
coord.st <- gobj@spatial_locs$cell$raw@coordinates
meta.st$sdimx <- coord.st$sdimx
meta.st$sdimy <- coord.st$sdimy

st.group.rename <- c("FADCtrl"="5xFAD-CTRL", "PLX5622"="5xFAD-PLX", "WTCtrl"="WT-CTRL")
colnames(meta.st)[colnames(meta.st) == "cell_types"] <- "brain_regions"
meta.st$treatment <- st.group.rename[meta.st$treatment]
meta.st$list_ID <- paste0(meta.st$treatment, "-", meta.st$batch)
umap <- read.csv("data/umap_harmony.csv",row.names=1)
colnames(umap) <- c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")
meta.st <- cbind(meta.st, umap)

saveRDS(meta.st, "data/meta.st.rds")
# meta.st <- readRDS("data/meta.st.rds")

# SN------------
library(Seurat)

sobj <- readRDS("../../002SC/001Seurat_out/001filter_and_integrate/sobj.rds")

## Prepare metadata SN ----
meta.sn <- sobj@meta.data
sc.group.rename <- c("AD_ctrl"="5xFAD-CTRL", "AD_5622"="5xFAD-PLX", "WT_ctrl"="WT-CTRL")
meta.sn$treatment <- sc.group.rename[meta.sn$treatment]
meta.sn <- meta.sn[, c("orig.ident", "treatment", "batch", "nCount_RNA", "nFeature_RNA", 
                       "percent.mt", "anno.L1", "anno.L2", "anno.L3")]
saveRDS(meta.sn, "data/meta.sn.rds")
# meta.sn <- readRDS("data/meta.sn.rds")
```

# Main figure plotting code

Newly generated figures are stored in the `publish_new` directory.

## Data preprocessing

```{r}
# env: thinkpad
# dir: C:/Users/a8799/Desktop/project/2022-10-14_mouse_spatial/manuscript

dir.create("data_new")

## Prepare metadata SN ----
meta.sn <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/sobj.meta.csv", row.names=1)
sc.group.rename <- c("AD_ctrl"="5xFAD-CTRL", "AD_5622"="5xFAD-PLX", "WT_ctrl"="WT-CTRL")
meta.sn$treatment <- sc.group.rename[meta.sn$treatment]
saveRDS(meta.sn, "data_new/meta.sn.rds")
# meta.sn <- readRDS("data_new/meta.sn.rds")
```

## Fig. 1c

```{r}
library(readxl)
data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure1C.xlsx")

data.long <- melt(data, id.vars = "Brain_region", variable.name = "treatment")
data.long$treatment <- sub("\\.\\.\\.\\d+$", "", data.long$treatment, perl=T)
data.long$treatment <- factor(data.long$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

upper.limit <- max(data.long$value)*1.3

if (F) {
  p <- ggplot(data.long, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
    geom_custom_barplot() + 
     stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("WT-CTRL", "5xFAD-CTRL"), 
                         c("5xFAD-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX")),
      method = "t.test",
      tip.length = signif_tip_length,
      label = "p.signif",
      family = "Arial", 
      size = signif_textsize, 
      bracket.size = signif_linewidth
    ) + 
    facet_wrap(.~Brain_region) + 
    labs(y="IBA1<sup>+</sup> load area (μm<sup>2</sup>)") + 
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, upper.limit)) + 
    theme(legend.position = "right", legend.title = element_blank())
} else {
  tukey <- data.long %>% 
    group_by(Brain_region) %>%
    tukey_hsd(value ~ treatment)
  
  tukey$comparison <- paste0(tukey$group1, "_vs_", tukey$group2)
  tukey$comparison <- factor(tukey$comparison, levels=c("WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-CTRL",
                                                        "5xFAD-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX",
                                                        "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX"), ordered=T)
  tukey  <- tukey[order(tukey$Brain_region, tukey$comparison), ]
  
  tukey <- tukey %>% add_xy_position(x = "treatment", step.increase = 0.12, )
  
  tukey <- subset(tukey, comparison != "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX")
  
      # stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0, 
      #                  family="Arial", size=signif_textsize, 
      #                  bracket.size = signif_linewidth) 
  p <- ggplot(data.long, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
    geom_custom_barplot() + 
    stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0, 
                           family="Arial", size=signif_textsize, 
                           bracket.size = signif_linewidth)  + 
    facet_wrap(.~Brain_region) + 
    labs(y="IBA1<sup>+</sup> load area (μm<sup>2</sup>)") + 
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, upper.limit)) + 
    theme(legend.position = "right", legend.title = element_blank())
}

ggsave("publish/fig1c.pdf", p, width=4.25, height=2.168, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig1c.png", p, width=4.25, height=2.168, dpi=300)


```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig1c.png")
```

## Fig. 1d 

```{r}

library(readxl)
data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure1D.xlsx")

data.long <- melt(data, id.vars = "Brain_region", variable.name = "treatment")
data.long$treatment <- sub("\\.\\.\\.\\d+$", "", data.long$treatment, perl=T)
data.long$treatment <- factor(data.long$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

upper.limit <- max(data.long$value)*1.3

p <- ggplot(data.long, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
   stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = list(c("5xFAD-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX")),
    method = "t.test",
    tip.length = signif_tip_length,
    label = "p.signif",
    family = "Arial", 
    size = signif_textsize, 
    bracket.size = signif_linewidth
  ) + 
  facet_wrap(.~Brain_region) + 
  labs(y="MOAB2<sup>+</sup> load area (μm<sup>2</sup>)") + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, upper.limit))
  
ggsave("publish/fig1d.pdf", p, width=2.865, height=2.168, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig1d.png", p, width=2.865, height=2.168, dpi=300)

```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig1d.png")
```

## Fig. 1f 

```{r}
library(readxl)
data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure1F.xlsx")

colnames(data) <- c("X", "Y") 

geom.color <- group.stroke["5xFAD-PLX"]

p <- ggplot(data, aes(X, Y)) + 
  geom_smooth(method="lm", linewidth=geom_linewidth, color=geom.color) + 
  geom_point(shape=21, size = 2, fill=NA, stroke = geom_linewidth, color=geom.color) + 
  stat_cor(method = "pearson", label.x.npc = "left", label.y.npc = "top", size=signif_textsize) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  labs(x="IBA1<sup>+</sup> cell area (μm<sup>2</sup>)", 
       y="6E10<sup>+</sup> plaque area (μm<sup>2</sup>)") +  
  theme_nature() + 
  theme(axis.title.x = element_markdown(), legend.position = "none", 
        axis.title.y = element_markdown())

ggsave("publish/fig1f.pdf", p, width=3.619, height=2.368, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig1f.png", p, width=3.619, height=2.368, dpi=300)
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig1f.png")
```

## Fig. 1g Lantency to platform (lineplot)

```{r}
library(readxl)
# Calculate mean, SD, SE, and CI for each time point and group
df_summary <- data.long %>%
  group_by(time, treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), # Mean
    SD = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE), # Standard Deviation
    N = n(), # Number of observations per group
    SE = SD / sqrt(N), # Standard Error
    CI_lower = Mean - 1.96 * SE, # 95% Confidence Interval lower limit (simple approximation)
    CI_upper = Mean + 1.96 * SE, # 95% Confidence Interval upper limit
    .groups = 'drop' # Complete grouping calculation
  )

# df_summary

p <- ggplot(df_summary, aes(x = time, y = Mean, color = treatment, group = treatment)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = geom_linewidth) + # Plot mean line
  geom_point(shape=21, size = 2, fill=NA, stroke = geom_linewidth) + # Optional: Add points at mean positions
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SE, 
                    ymax = Mean + SE), # Set error bar range (mean ± 1SE)
                width = 0.2, 
                linewidth = geom_linewidth) + # width controls error bar width
  labs(y = "Lantency to platform (s)") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group.fill) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = group.stroke) + 
  ylim(c(0, NA)) + 
  theme_nature() + 
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), 
        legend.position="top",
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        legend.direction = "horizontal")

ggsave("publish/fig1g.pdf", p, width=3.4078, height=2.4444, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig1g.png", p, width=3.4078, height=2.4444, dpi=300)
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig1g.png")
```

## Fig. 1h Lantency to platform (barplot)

```{r}

library(readxl)
data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 1H.xls")

data <- melt(data, variable.name = "treatment")
data <- na.omit(data)

data$treatment <- sub("×", "x", data$treatment)

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

if (F) {
  
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
    geom_custom_barplot() + 
     stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = list(c("WT-CTRL", "5xFAD-CTRL"), 
                         c("5xFAD-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX"), 
                         c("WT-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX")),
      method = "t.test",
      tip.length = signif_tip_length,
      label = "p.signif",
      family = "Arial", 
      size = signif_textsize, 
      bracket.size = signif_linewidth
    ) + 
    labs(y="Lantency to platform(s)") + 
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3))
} else {
    tukey <- data %>% 
#      group_by(Brain_region) %>%
      tukey_hsd(value ~ treatment)
    
    tukey$comparison <- paste0(tukey$group1, "_vs_", tukey$group2)
    tukey$comparison <- factor(tukey$comparison, levels=c("WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-CTRL",
                                                          "5xFAD-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX",
                                                          "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX"), ordered=T)
    tukey  <- tukey[order(tukey$comparison), ]
    
    tukey <- tukey %>% add_xy_position(x = "treatment", step.increase = 0.12, )
    
    # tukey <- subset(tukey, comparison != "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX")
  
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
    geom_custom_barplot() + 
    stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0,
                     family="Arial", size=signif_textsize,
                     bracket.size = signif_linewidth) + 
    labs(y="Lantency to platform(s)") + 
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3))
}

ggsave("publish/fig1h.pdf", p, width=1.991, height=2.264, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig1h.png", p, width=1.991, height=2.264, dpi=300)
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig1h.png")
```

## Fig. 1i

```{r}

library(readxl)
data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure1I.xlsx")

data <- melt(data, variable.name = "treatment")
data <- na.omit(data)

data$treatment <- sub("×", "x", data$treatment)

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

if (F) {
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
   stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = list(c("WT-CTRL", "5xFAD-CTRL"), 
                       c("5xFAD-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX"), 
                       c("WT-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX")),
    method = "t.test",
    tip.length = signif_tip_length,
    label = "p.signif",
    family = "Arial", 
    size = signif_textsize, 
    bracket.size = signif_linewidth
  ) + 
  labs(y="Target Crossing") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3))
  
} else {
  
    tukey <- data %>% 
#      group_by(Brain_region) %>%
      tukey_hsd(value ~ treatment)
    
    tukey$comparison <- paste0(tukey$group1, "_vs_", tukey$group2)
    tukey$comparison <- factor(tukey$comparison, levels=c("WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-CTRL",
                                                          "5xFAD-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX",
                                                          "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX"), ordered=T)
    tukey  <- tukey[order(tukey$comparison), ]
    
    tukey <- tukey %>% add_xy_position(x = "treatment", step.increase = 0.15, )
    
    # tukey <- subset(tukey, comparison != "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX")
  
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
    stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0,
                     family="Arial", size=signif_textsize,
                     bracket.size = signif_linewidth) + 
  labs(y="Target Crossing") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3))
}

  
ggsave("publish/fig1i.pdf", p, width=1.991, height=2.264, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig1i.png", p, width=1.991, height=2.264, dpi=300)

```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig1i.png")
```

## Fig. 2b

```{r}
library(readxl)

data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 2/FIgure 2B.xls")

data <- melt(data, variable.name = "treatment")
data <- na.omit(data)

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

tukey <- data %>% 
#      group_by(Brain_region) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ treatment)

tukey$comparison <- paste0(tukey$group1, "_vs_", tukey$group2)
tukey$comparison <- factor(tukey$comparison, levels=c("WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-CTRL",
                                                      "5xFAD-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX",
                                                      "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX"), ordered=T)
tukey  <- tukey[order(tukey$comparison), ]

tukey <- tukey %>% add_xy_position(x = "treatment", step.increase = 0.1)

p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
   stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0,
                     family="Arial", size=signif_textsize,
                     bracket.size = signif_linewidth) +  
  labs(y="IBA1<sup>+</sup> load area (μm<sup>2</sup>)") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma())

ggsave("publish/fig2b.pdf", p, width=2.4, height=2.5, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig2b.png", p, width=2.4, height=2.5, dpi=300)

```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig2b.png")
```

## Fig. 2c

```{r}

library(readxl)

data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 2/FIgure 2C.xls")

data <- melt(data, variable.name = "treatment")
data <- na.omit(data)

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
   stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = list(c("5xFAD-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX")),
    method = "t.test",
    tip.length = signif_tip_length,
    label = "p.signif",
    family = "Arial", 
    size = signif_textsize, 
    bracket.size = signif_linewidth
  ) + 
  labs(y="Moab2<sup>+</sup> load area (μm<sup>2</sup>)") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma())

ggsave("publish/fig2c.pdf", p, width=1.9, height=2.5, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig2c.png", p, width=1.9, height=2.5, dpi=300)

```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig2c.png")
```
## Fig. 2d

```{r}
library(readxl)
data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 2/FIgure 2D.xls")

data.long <- melt(data, id.vars = "time", variable.name = "treatment")

data.long$treatment <- sub("×", "x", data.long$treatment)

data.long$treatment <- sub("\\.\\.\\.\\d+$", "", data.long$treatment, perl=T)

data.long$treatment <- factor(data.long$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

df_summary <- data.long %>%
  group_by(time, treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), 
    SD = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE), 
    N = n(), # 
    SE = SD / sqrt(N), 
    CI_lower = Mean - 1.96 * SE, 
    CI_upper = Mean + 1.96 * SE, 
    .groups = 'drop' 
  )

# df_summary

p <- ggplot(df_summary, aes(x = time, y = Mean, color = treatment, group = treatment)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = geom_linewidth) + 
  geom_point(shape=21, size = 2, fill=NA, stroke = geom_linewidth) + 
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SE, 
                    ymax = Mean + SE), 
                width = 0.2, 
                linewidth = geom_linewidth) +
  labs(y = "Lantency to platform (s)") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group.fill) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = group.stroke) + 
  ylim(c(0, NA)) + 
  theme_nature() + 
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), 
        legend.position="top",
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        legend.direction = "horizontal")

ggsave("publish/fig2d.pdf", p, width=3.3385, height=2.379 , device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig2d.png", p, width=3.3385, height=2.379 , dpi=300)
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig2d.png")
```

## Fig. 2e

```{r}

data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 2/FIgure 2E.xls")

data <- melt(data, variable.name = "treatment")
data$treatment <- sub("×", "x", data$treatment)
data$treatment <- sub("5xFAD-PLX5622", "5xFAD-PLX", data$treatment)
data <- na.omit(data)

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

tukey <- data %>% 
#      group_by(Brain_region) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ treatment)

tukey$comparison <- paste0(tukey$group1, "_vs_", tukey$group2)
tukey$comparison <- factor(tukey$comparison, levels=c("WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-CTRL",
                                                      "5xFAD-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX",
                                                      "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX"), ordered=T)
tukey  <- tukey[order(tukey$comparison), ]

tukey <- tukey %>% add_xy_position(x = "treatment", step.increase = 0.09)

p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
      stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0,
                     family="Arial", size=signif_textsize,
                     bracket.size = signif_linewidth) + 
  labs(y="Lantency to platform (s)") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma())

ggsave("publish/fig2e.pdf", p, width=1.9967, height=2.379, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig2e.png", p, width=1.9967, height=2.379, dpi=300)

```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig2e.png")
```

## Fig. 2h

```{r}

data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 2/FIgure 2H.xls")

data <- melt(data, variable.name = "treatment")
data$treatment <- sub("×", "x", data$treatment)
data$treatment <- sub("5xFAD-PLX5622", "5xFAD-PLX", data$treatment)
data <- na.omit(data)

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
   stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = list(c("WT-CTRL", "5xFAD-CTRL"), 
                       c("5xFAD-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX"), 
                       c("WT-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX")),
    method = "t.test",
    tip.length = signif_tip_length,
    label = "p.signif",
    family = "Arial", 
    size = signif_textsize, 
    bracket.size = signif_linewidth
  ) + 
  labs(y="Freezing (%)") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma())

ggsave("publish/fig2h.pdf", p, width=2.1698, height=2.578, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig2h.png", p, width=2.1698, height=2.578, dpi=300)

```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig2h.png")
```

## Fig. 2i

```{r}

data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 2/FIgure 2i.xls")

data <- melt(data, variable.name = "treatment")
data$treatment <- sub("×", "x", data$treatment)
data$treatment <- sub("5xFAD-PLX5622", "5xFAD-PLX", data$treatment)
data <- na.omit(data)

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

tukey <- data %>% 
#      group_by(Brain_region) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ treatment)

tukey$comparison <- paste0(tukey$group1, "_vs_", tukey$group2)
tukey$comparison <- factor(tukey$comparison, levels=c("WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-CTRL",
                                                      "5xFAD-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX",
                                                      "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX"), ordered=T)
tukey  <- tukey[order(tukey$comparison), ]

tukey <- tukey %>% add_xy_position(x = "treatment", step.increase = 0.15)

p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
    geom_custom_barplot() + 
      stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0,
                     family="Arial", size=signif_textsize,
                     bracket.size = signif_linewidth) + 
  labs(y="Freezing (%)") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma())

ggsave("publish/fig2i.pdf", p, width=2.1698, height=2.578, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig2i.png", p, width=2.1698, height=2.578, dpi=300)

```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig2i.png")
```

## Fig. 2k

```{r}

data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 2/FIgure 2K.xls")

data <- melt(data, variable.name = "treatment")
data$treatment <- sub("×", "x", data$treatment)
data$treatment <- sub("5xFAD-PLX5622", "5xFAD-PLX", data$treatment)
data <- na.omit(data)

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

tukey <- data %>% 
#      group_by(Brain_region) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ treatment)

tukey$comparison <- paste0(tukey$group1, "_vs_", tukey$group2)
tukey$comparison <- factor(tukey$comparison, levels=c("WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-CTRL",
                                                      "5xFAD-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX",
                                                      "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX"), ordered=T)
tukey  <- tukey[order(tukey$comparison), ]

tukey <- tukey %>% add_xy_position(x = "treatment", step.increase = 0.4)

p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
  stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0,
               family="Arial", size=signif_textsize,
               bracket.size = signif_linewidth) + 
  labs(y="WT %<br>synaptophysin+PSD95<br>individual or Colocalized Puncta") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma())

ggsave("publish/fig2k.pdf", p, width=2.1698, height=2.578, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig2k.png", p, width=2.1698, height=2.578, dpi=300)

```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig2k.png")
```

## Fig. 3c

```{r}
library(readxl)
data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 3/Figure 3C.xls")

data.long <- melt(data, id.vars = "Brain_region", variable.name = "treatment")
data.long$treatment <- sub("\\.\\.\\.\\d+$", "", data.long$treatment, perl=T)
data.long$treatment <- factor(data.long$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

upper.limit <- max(data.long$value)*1.3

tukey <- data.long %>% 
  group_by(Brain_region) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ treatment)

tukey$comparison <- paste0(tukey$group1, "_vs_", tukey$group2)
tukey$comparison <- factor(tukey$comparison, levels=c("WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-CTRL",
                                                      "5xFAD-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX",
                                                      "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX"), ordered=T)
tukey  <- tukey[order(tukey$Brain_region, tukey$comparison), ]

tukey <- tukey %>% add_xy_position(x = "treatment", step.increase = 0.12, )

tukey <- subset(tukey, comparison != "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX")

p <- ggplot(data.long, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
    stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0, 
                           family="Arial", size=signif_textsize, 
                           bracket.size = signif_linewidth)  + 
  facet_wrap(.~Brain_region) + 
  labs(y="IBA1<sup>+</sup> load area (μm<sup>2</sup>)") + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, upper.limit)) + 
  theme(legend.position = "right", legend.title = element_blank())
  
ggsave("publish/fig3c.pdf", p, width=4.25, height=2.168, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig3c.png", p, width=4.25, height=2.168, dpi=300)
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig3c.png")
```

## Fig. 3d

```{r}
library(readxl)
data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 3/Figure 3D.xls")

data.long <- melt(data, id.vars = "Brain_region", variable.name = "treatment")
data.long$treatment <- sub("\\.\\.\\.\\d+$", "", data.long$treatment, perl=T)
data.long$treatment <- factor(data.long$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

upper.limit <- max(data.long$value)*1.3

p <- ggplot(data.long, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
   stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = list(c("5xFAD-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX")),
    method = "t.test",
    tip.length = signif_tip_length,
    label = "p.signif",
    family = "Arial", 
    size = signif_textsize, 
    bracket.size = signif_linewidth
  ) + 
  facet_wrap(.~Brain_region) + 
  labs(y="Moab2<sup>+</sup> load area (μm<sup>2</sup>)") + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, upper.limit))
  
ggsave("publish/fig3d.pdf", p, width=2.865, height=2.168, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig3d.png", p, width=2.865, height=2.168, dpi=300)
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig3d.png")
```

## Fig. 3e

```{r}
library(readxl)

data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 3/FIgure 3E.xls")

data <- melt(data, variable.name = "treatment")
data$treatment <- sub("×", "x", data$treatment)
data <- na.omit(data)

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

tukey <- data %>% 
#      group_by(Brain_region) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ treatment)

tukey$comparison <- paste0(tukey$group1, "_vs_", tukey$group2)
tukey$comparison <- factor(tukey$comparison, levels=c("WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-CTRL",
                                                      "5xFAD-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX",
                                                      "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX"), ordered=T)
tukey  <- tukey[order(tukey$comparison), ]

tukey <- tukey %>% add_xy_position(x = "treatment", step.increase = 0.2)

p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
      stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0,
                     family="Arial", size=signif_textsize,
                     bracket.size = signif_linewidth) + 
  labs(y="Alternation (%)") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma())

ggsave("publish/fig3e.pdf", p, width=2.4, height=2.5, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig3e.png", p, width=2.4, height=2.5, dpi=300)
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig3e.png")
```

## Fig. 3f

```{r}

library(readxl)
data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 3/Figure 3F.xls")

data.long <- melt(data, id.vars = "time", variable.name = "treatment")

data.long$treatment <- sub("×", "x", data.long$treatment)

data.long$treatment <- sub("\\.\\.\\.\\d+$", "", data.long$treatment, perl=T)

data.long$treatment <- factor(data.long$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

df_summary <- data.long %>%
  group_by(time, treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), 
    SD = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE), 
    N = n(), 
    SE = SD / sqrt(N), 
    CI_lower = Mean - 1.96 * SE, 
    CI_upper = Mean + 1.96 * SE, 
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# df_summary

p <- ggplot(df_summary, aes(x = time, y = Mean, color = treatment, group = treatment)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = geom_linewidth) + 
  geom_point(shape=21, size = 2, fill=NA, stroke = geom_linewidth) + 
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SE, 
                    ymax = Mean + SE), 
                width = 0.2, 
                linewidth = geom_linewidth) + 
  labs(y = "Lantency to platform (s)") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group.fill) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = group.stroke) + 
  ylim(c(0, NA)) + 
  theme_nature() + 
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), 
        legend.position="top",
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        legend.direction = "horizontal")

ggsave("publish/fig3f.pdf", p, width=3.3385, height=2.379 , device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig3f.png", p, width=3.3385, height=2.379 , dpi=300)

```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig3f.png")
```

## Fig. 3g

```{r}

library(readxl)

data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 3/FIgure 3G.xls")

data <- melt(data, variable.name = "treatment")
data$treatment <- sub("×", "x", data$treatment)
data <- na.omit(data)

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

tukey <- data %>% 
#      group_by(Brain_region) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ treatment)

tukey$comparison <- paste0(tukey$group1, "_vs_", tukey$group2)
tukey$comparison <- factor(tukey$comparison, levels=c("WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-CTRL",
                                                      "5xFAD-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX",
                                                      "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX"), ordered=T)
tukey  <- tukey[order(tukey$comparison), ]

tukey <- tukey %>% add_xy_position(x = "treatment", step.increase = 0.5)

p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
  stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0,
                     family="Arial", size=signif_textsize,
                     bracket.size = signif_linewidth) + 
  labs(y="Speed (cm/s)") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma())

ggsave("publish/fig3g.pdf", p, width=2.4, height=2.5, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig3g.png", p, width=2.4, height=2.5, dpi=300)

```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig3g.png")
```

## Fig. 3h

```{r}

library(readxl)

data <- read_excel("source_data/Figure 3/FIgure 3H.xls")

data <- melt(data, variable.name = "treatment")
data$treatment <- sub("×", "x", data$treatment)
data <- na.omit(data)

data$treatment <- factor(data$treatment, 
                         levels=names(group.fill), 
                         ordered=T)

tukey <- data %>% 
#      group_by(Brain_region) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ treatment)

tukey$comparison <- paste0(tukey$group1, "_vs_", tukey$group2)
tukey$comparison <- factor(tukey$comparison, levels=c("WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-CTRL",
                                                      "5xFAD-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX",
                                                      "WT-CTRL_vs_5xFAD-PLX"), ordered=T)
tukey  <- tukey[order(tukey$comparison), ]

tukey <- tukey %>% add_xy_position(x = "treatment", step.increase = 0.14)

p <- ggplot(data, aes(treatment, value, fill=treatment, color=treatment)) + 
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
  stat_pvalue_manual(tukey, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0,
                       family="Arial", size=signif_textsize,
                       bracket.size = signif_linewidth) + 
  labs(y="Time in target quadrant (%)") + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(data$value)*1.3)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma())

ggsave("publish/fig3h.pdf", p, width=2.4, height=2.5, device=cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig3h.png", p, width=2.4, height=2.5, dpi=300)

```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig3h.png")
```

## ---
## Fig. 4c: SN UMAP

Color palette

```{r}


# I have 7 major cell categories, among which GABA can be subdivided into 4 subtypes, Glu into 13 subtypes, Micro into 2 subtypes, and Astro into 3 subtypes. Now I need to draw a UMAP plot showing cell types. Please help me generate a color scheme where different major categories have large color differences, and cell subtypes use the same color system but have enough distinction.

# Load necessary packages
pak::pkg_install("scCustomize")
# pak::pkg_install("viridis")
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(viridis)
library(scales)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Create cell type color palette function
create_celltype_palette <- function() {
  # Main cell type colors (7 major categories)
  main_colors <- c(
    # "GABA" = "#6A0DAD",    # Purple
    # "Glu" = "#003F88",     # Dark blue
    "Micro" = "#A91B0D",   # Dark red
    "Astro" = "#2E8B57",   # Dark green
    "Oligo" = "#FF6D00",   # Orange
    "OPC" = "#00B4D8",     # Cyan
    "Endo" = "#FF5D8F"     # Pink
  )
  
  # GABA subtypes (4) - Purple gradient
  gaba_subtypes <- c(
    "GABA_0" = "#9B5DE5",
    "GABA_1" = "#C77DFF", 
    "GABA_2" = "#E0AAFF",
    "GABA_3" = "#F3E6FF"
  )
  
  glu_subtypes <- c(
    Glu_0 = "#8DD3C7",  # Cyan 1
    Glu_1 = "#FFFFB3",  # Yellow
    Glu_2 = "#BEBADA",  # Purple
    Glu_3 = "#FB8072",  # Pink
    Glu_4 = "#80B1D3",  # Light blue
    Glu_5 = "#FDB462",  # Orange
    Glu_6 = "#B3DE69",  # Yellow-green
    Glu_7 = "#FCCDE5",  # Pale pink
    Glu_8 = "#D9D9D9",  # Grey
    Glu_9 = "#BC80BD",  # Purple-red
    Glu_10 = "#CCEBC5",  # Light green
    Glu_11 = "#FFED6F",  # Golden yellow
    Glu_12 = "#A6CEE3"   # Blue
  )
  
  # # Glu subtypes (13) - Blue gradient
  # # Use the plasma palette from viridis to generate a blue-ish gradient
  # glu_colors <- viridis::plasma(13, begin = 0.1, end = 0.9, direction = -1)
  # glu_subtypes <- setNames(
  #   glu_colors,
  #   paste0("Glu_", 0:12)
  # )
  all_colors <- c(main_colors, gaba_subtypes, glu_subtypes)
  return(all_colors)
}
```


```{r}

# fig. 4c ----
# anno.L1 featurePlot ----
# env: thinkpad

meta.sn <- readRDS("data_new/meta.sn.rds")

L2.color <- create_celltype_palette()


# No legend
p <- ggplot(meta.sn, aes(UMAP_1, UMAP_2, color=anno.L2)) + 
  geom_point(size=.1) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = L2.color) + 
  theme_classic() + 
  theme(
    axis.line = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

ggsave(paste0("publish_new/fig4c_left.png"), p, width=3.3089, height=3.4057, dpi=300)

# knitr::include_graphics("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/umap.anno.L2.final.png")

## anno.L3 featurePlot ----
# env: thinkpad

for (ct in c("Micro", "Astro", "Oligo")) {
  # ct <- "Astro"
  sobj <- readRDS(paste0("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/cluster.L3/sobj.", ct, ".rds"))
  sobj$UMAP_1 <- NULL
  sobj$UMAP_2 <- NULL
  if (ct == "Micro") {
    sobj <- subset(sobj, idents=c(0, 1))
  } else if (ct == "Astro") {
    sobj <- subset(sobj, idents=c(0, 1))
  } else if (ct == "Oligo") {
    sobj <- subset(sobj, idents=c(0, 1, 2))
  }
  p <- DimPlot(sobj, pt.size = 0.1) + 
  theme_classic() + 
  theme(
    axis.line = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
  ggsave(paste0("publish_new/fig4c_right.", ct, ".png"), p, width=1.613, height=1.613, dpi=600) # legend: approx. 5/4
}
```

## Fig. 4d deconvo plot

Original fig. 4c right

```{r}
# fig. 4c right-------------------------------------------------
# Change GABA_0 to GABA_5 in the deconvolution image
# Required data: meta.sn.rds, deconvolution information
meta.st <- readRDS("data/meta.st.rds")

library(ggforce)
library(reshape2)

df.deconvo <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/cell2location2/anno.L2.spot.anno.prop.csv",
                       row.names=1)
row.names(df.deconvo) <- df.deconvo$cell.id
df.deconvo <- df.deconvo[meta.st$cell_ID, ]

interested_cts <- c("GABA_0", "Glu_0", "Micro", "Astro", "OPC", "Oligo")
meta.st <- cbind(meta.st, df.deconvo[, interested_cts])

# Plotting
# Image optimization:
# 1. Restore spots to true scale: 55. ok
# 2. Restore brain slices to true scale: coord_fix(). To use this parameter, coordinates must be corrected first. Get the center point coordinates of each sample,
#    then set the center point as the origin (0,0). AI can generate a function for this. ok
#    Correct coordinates:
#    1. Get the center of each sample.
#    2. Shift to 0.
# 3. Color: blue-white-red. Median is white. ok
# 6. Plot multiple cell subtypes. Merge them using patchwork.
# 4. Other styles kept consistent with the rest of the text.
# 5. Label brain regions

corrected_coord <- aggregate(cbind(sdimx, sdimy)~list_ID, FUN=median, data=meta.st)
data <- merge(meta.st, corrected_coord, by="list_ID", suffixes=c(".orgin", ".new"))
data$sdimx <- data$sdimx.orgin - data$sdimx.new
data$sdimy <- data$sdimy.orgin - data$sdimy.new

data.sub <- subset(data, list_ID == "5xFAD-CTRL-1")

p.list <- list()

# Fixed spot size, requires geom_circle, which is slow.
for (ct in interested_cts) {
  data.ct <- data.sub[, c("sdimx", "sdimy", ct)]
  colnames(data.ct) <- c("sdimx", "sdimy", "abundance")
  if (ct == "Micro_0") ct <- "HOM"
  if (ct == "Micro_1") ct <- "DAM"
  # max.expr <- max(data.ct$abundance)
  # median.expr <- median(data.ct$abundance)
  data.ct.summary <- boxplot(data.ct$abundance, plot = F)$stats
  upper <- data.ct.summary[5]
  middle <- data.ct.summary[3]
  lower <- data.ct.summary[1]
  data.ct$abundance[data.ct$abundance > upper] <- upper
  data.ct$abundance[data.ct$abundance < lower] <- lower
  p <- ggplot(data.ct, aes(sdimx, sdimy)) + 
    geom_point(aes(color=abundance), size=.05) + 
    # geom_circle(aes(x0 = sdimx, y0 = sdimy, r=55, fill=abundance), color = NA) +
    scale_color_gradientn(colours=c("blue", "white", "red"), 
                         values=scales::rescale(c(lower, middle, upper))) + 
    labs(title=ct) + 
    coord_fixed() + 
    theme_nature() + 
    theme(panel.background=element_blank(), panel.grid=element_blank(),
          axis.title=element_blank(), 
          axis.text=element_blank(), 
          axis.ticks=element_blank(), 
          legend.position = "none", 
          axis.line.x = element_blank(), 
          axis.line.y = element_blank(), 
          plot.title = element_blank())
  
  p.list[[ct]] <- p
}

pall <- patchwork::wrap_plots(p.list, ncol=2)

ggsave("publish_new/Fig4d.png", width=3.5, height=3.5 * 785/632, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish_new/Fig4d.png")
```

## Fig. 4e Micro proportion

```{r}

pairs <- list()
pairs[[1]] <- c("AD_5622", "AD_ctrl")
pairs[[2]] <- c("AD_5622", "WT_ctrl")
pairs[[3]] <- c("AD_ctrl", "WT_ctrl")


CT <- "Micro"
label <- "anno.L2"
data <- data.frame()

for (pair in pairs) {
  S1 <- pair[1]
  S2 <- pair[2]
  infile <- paste0("../002SC/004Seurat_out/003proportion/scProportionTest/", 
                   label, ".", S1, "_vs_", S2, ".csv")
  df <- read.csv(infile)
  df <- subset(df, clusters == CT, select=c("obs_log2FD", "FDR", "boot_mean_log2FD", "boot_CI_2.5", "boot_CI_97.5"))
  df$comparison <- paste0(S1, "_vs_", S2)
  data <- rbind(data, df)
}

comparison.rename <- c("AD_5622_vs_AD_ctrl"="PL_vs_AD", 
                       "AD_ctrl_vs_WT_ctrl"="AD_vs_WT", 
                       "AD_5622_vs_WT_ctrl"="PL_vs_WT")

data$comparison <- comparison.rename[data$comparison]

data$comparison <- factor(data$comparison, levels=c("AD_vs_WT", 
                                                    "PL_vs_AD", 
                                                    "PL_vs_WT"),
                          ordered=T)

data$point.color <- ifelse(abs(data$obs_log2FD) > log2(1.2) & data$FDR<0.05,
                           "|FC| > 1.2 & FDR < 0.05", "NS")

p <- ggplot(data, aes(comparison, obs_log2FD)) +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin=boot_CI_2.5, ymax=boot_CI_97.5)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept=-log2(1.2), color="grey", linetype="dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept=log2(1.2), color="grey", linetype="dashed") +
  geom_point(aes(color=point.color)) + 
  labs(title="", y="Proportion Change<br>(Log<sub>2</sub> Fold Change)") + 
  scale_color_manual(values=c("|FC| > 1.2 & FDR < 0.05"="red", "NS"="black")) + 
  theme_nature() + 
  theme(axis.title.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle=30, vjust = 0.7, hjust=.7),
        legend.position = "none",
        axis.title.y=element_markdown())

ggsave("publish_new/fig4e.pdf", p, width=1.4861, height=1.8333, device = cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish_new/fig4e.png", p, width=1.4861, height=1.8333, dpi=300)

```

## Fig. 4f: ST Microglia expression ~ brain region + treatment

```{r}
# Original Fig 4e Microglia expression in each brain region and treatment --------------------------
# Focus on changes in microglia across different brain regions between treatments without distinguishing subtypes.

# env: dhlab
setwd("/home/mouse_ad/001ST/")
gobj <- readRDS("001Giotto_out/testcombo.rds")

if (F) {
  # Use non-log normalized data when Fold Change/depletion rate is needed.
  gobj <- Giotto::normalizeGiotto(gobj, log_norm=F)
}

gobj.expr <- get_expression_values(gobj, values="normalized") 

gobj.meta <- pDataDT(gobj)
interested.genes <- c("C1qa", "Gfap", "P2ry12", "Trem2")
interested.expr <- t(as.matrix(gobj.expr@exprMat[gobj@feat_ID$rna %in% interested.genes, ]))
colnames(interested.expr) <- interested.genes
gobj.meta <- cbind(gobj.meta, interested.expr)

# gobj.meta$C1qa <- as.vector(gobj.expr@exprMat["C1qa", ]) 
# gobj.meta$C1qa <- as.vector(gobj.expr@exprMat["Gfap", ]) 
meta.immuno <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta_GFAP_P2RY12.csv", row.names=1)
gobj.meta$Abeta_index <- meta.immuno$Abeta_index

write.csv(gobj.meta, "001Giotto_out/microglia_distribution/gobj.meta.log_normalized_expr.csv")

# env: thinkpad
gobj.meta <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/microglia_distribution/gobj.meta.log_normalized_expr.csv", row.names=1)

gobj.meta.agg <- aggregate(C1qa~treatment+cell_types+batch, FUN=mean, data=gobj.meta)
gobj.meta.agg <- subset(gobj.meta.agg, ! cell_types %in% c("Lateral Ventricle", "Third Ventricle", "C12"))

rename_group <- c("FADCtrl" = "5xFAD-CTRL",
                  "PLX5622" = "5xFAD-PLX",
                  "WTCtrl" = "WT-CTRL")
gobj.meta.agg$treatment <- rename_group[gobj.meta.agg$treatment]

gobj.meta.agg$treatment <- factor(gobj.meta.agg$treatment, levels=c("WT-CTRL", "5xFAD-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX"), 
                                      ordered=T)

p <- ggplot(gobj.meta.agg, aes(treatment, C1qa, color = treatment, fill = treatment)) +
  geom_custom_barplot() + 
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = list(c("WT-CTRL", "5xFAD-CTRL"), c("5xFAD-CTRL", "5xFAD-PLX")),
    method = "t.test",
    tip.length = signif_tip_length,
    label = "p.signif",
    family = "Arial", 
    size = signif_textsize, 
    bracket.size = axis_linewidth
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "<b><i>C1qa</i> expression<br>(Log-normalized counts)</b>") +
  facet_wrap(. ~ cell_types, ncol = 5) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(NA, max(gobj.meta.agg$C1qa) * 1.3))

ggsave.plus("fig4f", p, 5.5, 2.5)

# write.csv(p@data, "source_data/fig4e.csv", row.names=F)
# ggsave("publish/fig4e.png", p, width=5.5, height=2.5, dpi=300)
# ggsave("publish/fig4e.pdf", p, width=5.5, height=2.5, device=cairo_pdf)
```


```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig4e.png")
```

## ---

## Fig. 4 Ext. a Micro ST DEGs heatmap

```{r}
# 参考：
# C:/Users/a8799/Desktop/project/2022-10-14_mouse_spatial/PLX5622/003ST_DEGs_spot.Rmd#st-degs-heatmap-pdf
```


## Fig. 4 Ext. b,c brain slice heatmap

updated: 2026-01-20

- b: n DEGs
- c: Aβ load

```{r}
# Necessary libraries
library(xml2)       # Alternative to fluctuator, standard XML/SVG processing
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)     # Used for professional color mapping
library(cowplot)
library(magick)
library(rsvg)
library(dplyr)

# Optimized spatial.heatmap function
spatial.heatmap <- function(value.df, 
                            out.prefix, 
                            template.path = "data/template_no_legend.svg", # Use template path as parameter
                            scale.method = "linear", # linear or log
                            colormap = c("blue", "white", "red"),
                            na.color = "#eeeeee") { # Color for missing values
  
  # 1. Data preprocessing
  # -------------------------------------------------------
  # Ensure uniform input format: first column is brain region, second column is value
  names(value.df)[1:2] <- c("brain_region", "value")
  
  # Simple normalization logic (optimized based on scale.fun2 logic)
  # If log2FC truncation logic is needed, it's recommended to process data before passing it in to keep the function pure.
  plot.data <- value.df
  
  # 2. Color Mapping
  # -------------------------------------------------------
  # Use the scales package for accurate color mapping meeting publishing standards
  if (length(colormap) == 3) {
    # Suitable for Diverging data (e.g., log2FC: blue-white-red)
    limit_max <- max(abs(plot.data$value), na.rm = T)
    col_func <- scales::col_numeric(palette = colormap, 
                                    domain = c(-limit_max, limit_max))
    # Ensure the legend is symmetrical
    legend_limits <- c(-limit_max, limit_max)
  } else {
    # Suitable for Sequential data (e.g., Counts: white-red)
    col_func <- scales::col_numeric(palette = colormap, 
                                    domain = range(plot.data$value, na.rm = T))
    legend_limits <- range(plot.data$value, na.rm = T)
  }
  
  # Assign color to each brain region
  plot.data$color_hex <- col_func(plot.data$value)
  
  # 3. SVG manipulation (based on xml2)
  # -------------------------------------------------------
  if (!file.exists(template.path)) stop("SVG template not found!")
  
  svg_doc <- read_xml(template.path)
  
  # Get all nodes with ids in the SVG (assuming brain region names correspond to SVG ids)
  # Note: SVG namespaces can be troublesome; use wildcards to simplify.
  all_nodes <- xml_find_all(svg_doc, "//*[@id]")
  svg_ids <- xml_attr(all_nodes, "id")
  
  # Loop through data to fill colors
  matched_count <- 0
  for (i in 1:nrow(plot.data)) {
    region <- plot.data$brain_region[i]
    color <- plot.data$color_hex[i]
    
    # Handle spaces in brain region names (assuming SVG ids use underscores instead of spaces)
    region_id <- gsub(" ", "_", region)
    
    # Find corresponding node
    target_node <- all_nodes[svg_ids == region_id]
    
    if (length(target_node) > 0) {
      # Rigorous approach: modify the fill in the style attribute
      # Directly set the fill attribute to override style settings (attribute usually has higher priority)
      # Or parse and replace the style string. We force-add the fill attribute for compatibility.
      xml_set_attr(target_node, "fill", color)
      xml_set_attr(target_node, "style", paste0("fill:", color, ";")) # Double insurance
      matched_count <- matched_count + 1
    } else {
      warning(paste("Brain region not found in SVG:", region))
    }
  }
  
  message(paste("Mapped", matched_count, "regions out of", nrow(plot.data), "input regions."))
  
  # Save modified SVG
  svg_out_path <- paste0(out.prefix, ".svg")
  write_xml(svg_doc, svg_out_path)
  
  # 4. Generate Legend
  # -------------------------------------------------------
  # Create an invisible dummy plot specifically for extracting the legend
  p_dummy <- ggplot(data.frame(x = 1, y = plot.data$value), aes(x, y, fill = y)) +
    geom_col() +
    theme_void() 
    
  if (length(colormap) == 3) {
    p_dummy <- p_dummy + 
      scale_fill_gradient2(low = colormap[1], mid = colormap[2], high = colormap[3], 
                           midpoint = 0, limits = legend_limits, name = names(value.df)[2])
  } else {
    p_dummy <- p_dummy + 
      scale_fill_gradientn(colors = colormap, limits = legend_limits, name = names(value.df)[2])
  }
  
  my_legend <- get_legend(p_dummy)
  
  # 5. Combine final image (PDF)
  # -------------------------------------------------------
  # Read modified SVG as bitmap/raster (for typesetting in ggplot)
  # Note: Use grImport2 for ultimate vector graphics, but magick is sufficient for most journals.
  svg_bitmap <- image_read_svg(svg_out_path, width = 1000) # Increase resolution
  
  final_plot <- ggdraw() +
    draw_image(svg_bitmap, x = 0, y = 0, width = 0.8, height = 1) + # Brain map on the left
    draw_plot(my_legend, x = 0.8, y = 0.4, width = 0.2, height = 0.2) # Legend on the right
  
  ggsave(paste0(out.prefix, ".pdf"), final_plot, width = 8, height = 6)
  message(paste("Output saved to:", paste0(out.prefix, ".pdf")))
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Test code (based on your data structure)
# -----------------------------------------------------------

# Simulated data (assuming you have read st.DEGs.agg.sub)
st.DEGs.agg.sub <- data.frame(brain_region = c("Cortex", "Thalamus", "Hippocampus"), gene = c(10, 50, 20))

# Run function
# Note: Ensure the template_no_legend.svg file exists
spatial.heatmap(st.DEGs.agg.sub,
                out.prefix = "publish_new/test",
                template.path = "data/template_no_legend.svg",
                colormap = c("white", "red"))


# n DEGs
# prepare data
st.DEGs <- read.csv("data/st.DEGs.csv", row.names=1)
st.DEGs.agg <- aggregate(gene~brain_region+comparison, FUN=length, data=st.DEGs)
st.DEGs.agg <- st.DEGs.agg[order(st.DEGs.agg$comparison, st.DEGs.agg$gene), ]
st.DEGs.agg.sub <- subset(st.DEGs.agg, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")


spatial.heatmap(st.DEGs.agg.sub[, c(1,3)],
                out.prefix = "publish_new/fig4Ext.b",
                template.path = "data/template_no_legend.svg",
                colormap = c("white", "red"))

# Aβ load
Abeta.merged <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/delta_Abeta.csv", row.names=1)

spatial.heatmap(Abeta.merged[, c("brain_region", "AD_vs_WT")],
                out.prefix = "publish_new/fig4Ext.c",
                template.path = "data/template_no_legend.svg",
                colormap = c("white", "red"))
```

## Fig. 4 Ext. d: ST DEGs enrichment heatmap

Reference: Original Fig. 4 Ext. b: ST DEGs pathway enrichment heatmap

1. Remove neurodegenerative disease pathways.
2. Remove ellipses.
3. Synapse pruning pathway mentioned in the text must be added. Other mentioned pathways as well.

```{r}

df.selected <- read.csv("./data/st.DEGs.agg_PL_vs_AD.txt_custom_bg/Enrichment_heatmap/HeatmapSelectedGO.csv")
df.all <- read.csv("./data/st.DEGs.agg_PL_vs_AD.txt_custom_bg/Enrichment_GO/GO_AllLists.csv")

df.selected2 <- subset(df.all, GO %in% df.selected$GO)

library(reshape2)

df.selected2.mat <- dcast(Description~GeneList, value.var="LogP", data=df.selected2) # Inspection complete, basically consistent with df.selected.

excluded <- c("mmu05022", # Pathways of neurodegeneration - multiple diseases - Mus musculus (house mouse)
              "mmu00190", # Oxidative phosphorylation - Mus musculus (house
              "mmu04932", # Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease - Mus musculus
              "R-MMU-3371497",# HSP90 chaperone cycle for steroid hormone receptors
              "GO:0002478" # antigen processing and presentation of exogenous peptide
              )
included <- c("GO:0098883", # "Synapse pruning", 
              "GO:0006119", # "Oxidative phos"
              "GO:0019882" # antigen processing and presentation
              )

GO.selected <- union(setdiff(df.selected$GO, excluded), included)
df.selected3 <- subset(df.all, GO %in% GO.selected)

df.selected3.mat <- dcast(Description~GeneList, value.var="LogP", data=df.selected3) 
df.selected3.mat[is.na(df.selected3.mat)] <- 0

df.selected3.mat <- data.frame(df.selected3.mat[, -1], 
                               row.names=sub(" - Mus musculus (house mouse)", "", 
                                             df.selected3.mat$Description, fixed = T), 
                               check.names = F)

polished_row <- c(
  "antigen processing and presentation" = "Antigen processing and presentation", 
  "Glycolysis and gluconeogenesis" = "Glycolysis and gluconeogenesis", 
  "learning or memory" = "Learning or memory", 
  "MHC class II antigen presentation" = "MHC class II antigen presentation", 
  "Microglia pathogen phagocytosis pathway" = "Microglia pathogen phagocytosis pathway", 
  "myeloid leukocyte activation" = "Myeloid leukocyte activation", 
  "Neutrophil degranulation" = "Neutrophil degranulation", 
  "oxidative phosphorylation" = "Oxidative phosphorylation", 
  "Phagosome" = "Phagosome", 
  "regulation of cell activation" = "Regulation of cell activation", 
  "regulation of synaptic plasticity" = "Regulation of synaptic plasticity", 
  "regulation of vesicle-mediated transport" = "Regulation of vesicle-mediated transport", 
  "synapse organization" = "Synapse organization", 
  "synapse pruning" = "Synapse pruning", 
  "synaptic vesicle recycling" = "Synaptic vesicle recycling", 
  "Translation" = "Translation", 
  "Transmission across Chemical Synapses" = "Transmission across chemical synapses", 
  "vesicle-mediated transport in synapse" = "Vesicle-mediated transport in synapse"
  )

row.names(df.selected3.mat) <- polished_row[row.names(df.selected3.mat)]

colnames(df.selected3.mat) <- sub("Olfactory area", "Olfactory cortical area", colnames(df.selected3.mat))

library(pheatmap)

my_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("grey90", "white", "firebrick3"))(100)
breaks <- c(seq(0, 2, length.out = 51), 
            seq(2.0001, 20, length.out = 51))

df.selected3.mat = -df.selected3.mat
df.selected3.mat[df.selected3.mat > 20] <- 20

p.obj <- pheatmap(df.selected3.mat, silent = T)

df.selected3.mat <- df.selected3.mat[p.obj$tree_row$order, p.obj$tree_col$order]

p.obj <- pheatmap(df.selected3.mat, fontsize = 8, cluster_rows = F, cluster_cols = F, 
         color = my_colors,
         breaks = breaks)

pdf("publish_new/fig4Ext.d.pdf", width=4.71, height=3.32)

grid::grid.draw(p.obj)

dev.off()

png("publish_new/fig4Ext.d.png", width=4.71, height=3.32, units = "in", res=300)

grid::grid.draw(p.obj)

dev.off()
```

## Fig. 4 Ext. e: Protein co-expression module

Reference: data/st.DEGs.agg_PL_vs_AD.txt_custom_bg/. After selecting specific modules, manually draw using Adobe Illustrator.

## Fig. 4 Ext. f: PIGs (PL vs AD)

todo: Modify downstream chart numbers.

```{r}
# fig. 4 ext. d: PIGs----
PIGs.df <- read.csv("data/PIGs.lfc_0.1.csv", row.names=1)
FADCtrl.PIGs <- intersect(PIGs.df[PIGs.df$sample=="FADCtrl_1", "feats"], 
                          PIGs.df[PIGs.df$sample=="FADCtrl_2", "feats"])
PLX5622.PIGs <- intersect(PIGs.df[PIGs.df$sample=="PLX5622_1", "feats"], 
                          PIGs.df[PIGs.df$sample=="PLX5622_2", "feats"])
WTCtrl.PIGs <- intersect(PIGs.df[PIGs.df$sample=="WTCtrl_1", "feats"], 
                         PIGs.df[PIGs.df$sample=="WTCtrl_2", "feats"])
length(FADCtrl.PIGs) # 13
length(PLX5622.PIGs) # 16
PL.inter.AD <- intersect(FADCtrl.PIGs, PLX5622.PIGs)
FADCtrl.specific <- setdiff(FADCtrl.PIGs, PLX5622.PIGs)
PLX5622.specific <- setdiff(PLX5622.PIGs, FADCtrl.PIGs)

# Label genes

PIGs <- data.frame(gene=c(sort(FADCtrl.specific), 
                          sort(PL.inter.AD), 
                          sort(PLX5622.specific)))

# AD dysregulated genes (RNASeq)
AD.dysregulated.genes <- read.csv("data/AD_sc_dysregulated_genes.mouseID.csv", row.names=1)
AD.dysregulated.genes.list <- unique(unlist(strsplit(AD.dysregulated.genes$geneList, ";")))
PIGs$AD.dysregulated <- PIGs$gene %in% AD.dysregulated.genes.list

# AD related genes (literature)
AD.related.genes <- read.csv("data/AD_related_genes.mouse.csv", row.names=1)
PIGs$AD.related.genes <- PIGs$gene %in% AD.related.genes$Mouse.symbol

# AD risk genes (GWAS)
AD.risk.genes <- read.csv("data/AD_var_from_GWAS.csv", skip = 2)
AD.risk.genes.human <- unique(AD.risk.genes$Gene)

# Convert to mouse symbol
library(homologene)
out <- human2mouse(AD.risk.genes.human)
human2mouse.map <- out$mouseGene
names(human2mouse.map) <- out$humanGene
AD.risk.genes$Gene.mouse <- human2mouse.map[AD.risk.genes$Gene]
write.csv(AD.risk.genes, "data/AD_var_from_GWAS.mouse.csv", row.names=F)
# Manually fill in the missing data using the ortholog section of GeneCards.
# Save as AD_var_from_GWAS.mouse.completed.csv.
AD.risk.genes2 <- read.csv("data/AD_var_from_GWAS.mouse.completed.csv")
PIGs$AD.risk.genes <- PIGs$gene %in% AD.risk.genes2$Gene.mouse

# ST DEGs
st.DEGs <- read.csv("data/st.DEGs.csv", row.names = 1)
drug_effect_genes <- subset(st.DEGs, comparison == "PLX5622_vs_FADCtrl")
PIGs$drug_effect_genes <- PIGs$gene %in% drug_effect_genes$gene

library(pheatmap)
pdf("publish/fig4.ext.d.heatmap.pdf")
pheatmap(data.frame(apply(PIGs[, -1], 2, as.numeric), row.names=PIGs$gene), 
         cluster_rows = F, cluster_cols = F, color = c("grey", "black"), 
         cellwidth=10, cellheight = 10)
dev.off()
```


## ---

## Fig. 5a: Aβ~C1qa change

```{r}
# env: dhlab
setwd("/home/mouse_ad/001ST/")
gobj <- readRDS("001Giotto_out/testcombo.rds")

if (T) {
  # Use non-log normalized data when Fold Change/depletion rate is needed.
  gobj <- Giotto::normalizeGiotto(gobj, log_norm=F)
}

gobj.expr <- get_expression_values(gobj, values="normalized") 

gobj.meta <- pDataDT(gobj)
interested.genes <- c("C1qa", "Gfap", "P2ry12", "Trem2")
interested.expr <- t(as.matrix(gobj.expr@exprMat[gobj@feat_ID$rna %in% interested.genes, ]))
colnames(interested.expr) <- interested.genes
gobj.meta <- cbind(gobj.meta, interested.expr)

# gobj.meta$C1qa <- as.vector(gobj.expr@exprMat["C1qa", ]) 
# gobj.meta$C1qa <- as.vector(gobj.expr@exprMat["Gfap", ]) 
meta.immuno <- read.csv("001Giotto_out/010immunofluorescence/merged/meta.Abeta_GFAP_P2RY12.csv", row.names=1)
gobj.meta$Abeta_index <- meta.immuno$Abeta_index

if (T) {
  write.csv(gobj.meta, "001Giotto_out/microglia_distribution/gobj.meta.not_log_normalized_expr.csv")
}

# env: thinkpad
gobj.meta <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/microglia_distribution/gobj.meta.not_log_normalized_expr.csv", row.names=1)

C1qa.AD_vs_WT <- gobj.meta %>%
  filter(treatment != "PLX5622") %>%
  # Calculate mean by treatment, batch, cell_types
  group_by(treatment, batch, cell_types) %>%
  summarise(
    C1qa = mean(C1qa, na.rm = TRUE),
    Abeta_index = mean(Abeta_index, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Pivot AD and WT to wide format
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = treatment,
    values_from = c(C1qa, Abeta_index),
    names_sep = "."
  ) %>%
  # Calculate derived indicators
  mutate(
    C1qa.FC.AD = C1qa.FADCtrl/ C1qa.WTCtrl,
    Abeta_index.AD = Abeta_index.FADCtrl - Abeta_index.WTCtrl, 
  ) %>%
  # Filter specific cell types
  filter(!cell_types %in% c("C12", "Lateral Ventricle", "Third Ventricle"))

# write.csv(C1qa.AD_vs_WT, "001Giotto_out/Abeta_and_depletion_rate/C1qa.AD_vs_WT.csv")
# C1qa.AD_vs_WT <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/Abeta_and_depletion_rate/C1qa.AD_vs_WT.csv", row.names=1)

# 2. Complete mean and standard error calculation in one step
C1qa.AD_vs_WT.agg <- C1qa.AD_vs_WT %>%
  group_by(cell_types) %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      c(C1qa.FC.AD,Abeta_index.AD),
      list(
        mean = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        sem = ~sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n())
      )
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

C1qa.PL_vs_AD <- gobj.meta %>%
  filter(treatment != "WTCtrl") %>%
  group_by(treatment, batch, cell_types) %>%
  summarise(
    C1qa = mean(C1qa, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = treatment,
    values_from = c(C1qa),
    names_sep = "."
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    C1qa.FC.PL = PLX5622/ FADCtrl
  ) %>%
  filter(!cell_types %in% c("C12", "Lateral Ventricle", "Third Ventricle"))

# write.csv(C1qa.PL_vs_AD, "001Giotto_out/Abeta_and_depletion_rate/C1qa.PL_vs_AD.csv")
# C1qa.PL_vs_AD <- read.csv("../001ST/001Giotto_out/Abeta_and_depletion_rate/C1qa.PL_vs_AD.csv", row.names=1)

C1qa.PL_vs_AD <- merge(C1qa.AD_vs_WT[, c("batch", "cell_types", "Abeta_index.AD")], 
                       C1qa.PL_vs_AD[, c("batch", "cell_types", "C1qa.FC.PL")],
                       by=c("batch", "cell_types"))

C1qa.PL_vs_AD.agg <- C1qa.PL_vs_AD %>%
  group_by(cell_types) %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      c(C1qa.FC.PL, Abeta_index.AD),
      list(
        mean = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        sem = ~sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n())
      )
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )


mycolorcode = c("Isocortex"="#666666",
                "Fiber tracts"="#E7298A",
                "Thalamus"="#FF7F00",
                "Olfactory area"="#E41A1C",
                "Hypothalamus"="#377EB8",
                "Hippocampus"="#4DAF4A",
                "Cortical subplate"="#A65628",
                "Meninges"="#80B1D3",
                "Striatum"="#B3DE69")


# use facet
colnames(C1qa.AD_vs_WT.agg) <- c("cell_types", "FC", "FC_sem", "Abeta_index.AD", "Abeta_index.AD_sem")
colnames(C1qa.PL_vs_AD.agg) <- c("cell_types", "FC", "FC_sem", "Abeta_index.AD", "Abeta_index.AD_sem")
C1qa.AD_vs_WT.agg$comparison <- "5xFAD-CTRL vs WT-CTRL"
C1qa.PL_vs_AD.agg$comparison <- "5xFAD-PLX vs 5xFAD-CTRL"

c1qa.agg <- rbind(C1qa.AD_vs_WT.agg, C1qa.PL_vs_AD.agg)

# horizontal distribution
# To ensure a consistent point size of 2, use geom_point instead of geom_pointrange.
p <- ggplot(c1qa.agg, aes(Abeta_index.AD, FC)) + 
  # geom_pointrange(aes(ymin=FC-FC_sem, 
  #                     ymax=FC+FC_sem, 
  #                     color=cell_types), 
  #                 size=1.5, 
  #                 linewidth = geom_linewidth) + 
  geom_smooth(method="lm", linewidth=geom_linewidth) + 
  geom_point(aes(color=cell_types), shape=21, size = 2, fill=NA, stroke = geom_linewidth) + # 可选：在均值位置添加点
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = FC-FC_sem, 
                    ymax = FC+FC_sem, 
                    color=cell_types), 
                width = 0.2, 
                linewidth = geom_linewidth) + 
  stat_cor(method = "pearson", label.x.npc = "left", label.y.npc = "top", size=signif_textsize) + 
  scale_color_manual(name="Brain Region", values = mycolorcode) + 
  labs(x="Mean Aβ Index", y="<b>Fold Change of <i>C1qa</i></b>") +  
  facet_wrap(.~comparison, scale="free_y") + 
  theme_nature() + 
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), 
        axis.title.y=element_markdown())

ggsave("publish/fig4ext.d.pdf", p, width=5.0978, height=2.1409, device = cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish/fig4ext.d.png", p, width=5.0978, height=2.1409, dpi=300)
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("publish/fig4ext.d.png")
```


## Fig. 5b Microglia subtype classification

Reference: Fig. 8h

```{r}
# env: thinkpad

library(Seurat)

sobj.sub <- readRDS("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/cluster.L3/sobj.Micro.rds")
Idents(sobj.sub) <- "nn_res.0.3"

sobj.sub <- subset(sobj.sub, idents=c(0, 1))

df.marker <- FindAllMarkers(sobj.sub, only.pos = T)

df.top10 <- df.marker %>% group_by(cluster) %>% slice_head(n=10)

df.top10

#       p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj cluster gene    
#       <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> <fct>   <chr>   
#  1 7.03e-45      1.22  0.902 0.73   1.77e-40 0       Zfhx3   
#  ... (results summary)

# Some genes might still belong to ambient RNA.
cell.DEGs <- read.csv("../002SC/004Seurat_out/002cell_type_anno/anno.L1.FindAllMarkers.csv", row.names=1)
cell.DEGs.sub <- subset(cell.DEGs, cluster=="Micro")
ambient_genes <- readLines("../002SC/004Seurat_out/001filter_and_integrate/decontx/ambient.genes.list")
df.top10$is.ambient <- df.top10$gene %in% ambient_genes
df.top10$is.Micro_marker <- df.top10$gene %in% cell.DEGs.sub$gene

df.top10 <- df.top10 %>% filter(!(is.ambient & !is.Micro_marker))

hom.marker <- c("Elmo1", "Selplg", "Srgap2", "Tmem119")
dam.marker <- c("Apoe", "Igf1", "Myo1e", "Trem2")

p <- DotPlot(sobj.sub, c(hom.marker, dam.marker)) + 
  coord_flip() + 
  theme_nature() + 
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c("HOM", "DAM")) + 
  theme(legend.title = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.y = element_text(face="italic"), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank())

ggsave("publish_new/figS4e.pdf", p, width=2.4077, height = 2.2, device = cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish_new/figS4e.png", p, width=2.4077, height = 2.2, dpi=300)
```

## Fig. 5c DAM proportion

- Proportion change relative to Micro.
- Proportion change relative to all cell types. An analysis of anno.L3 cell proportions is still needed.

The proportion change of a specific cell type across three combinations can be abstracted into a function. Specify the subtype of interest and the permutationplot prefix.

Define plotting function

```{r}

# ../002SC/004Seurat_out/003proportion/scProportionTest_subtype
library(ggplot2)

permutationPlot.target_ct <- function(CT, Title, in.prefix) {
  pairs <- list()
  pairs[[1]] <- c("AD_5622", "AD_ctrl")
  pairs[[2]] <- c("AD_5622", "WT_ctrl")
  pairs[[3]] <- c("AD_ctrl", "WT_ctrl")
  
  data <- data.frame()

  # Get statistical results for specific cell types in three comparison combinations.
  for (pair in pairs) {
    S1 <- pair[1]
    S2 <- pair[2]
    infile <- paste0(in.prefix, ".", S1, "_vs_", S2, ".csv")
    df <- read.csv(infile)
    df <- subset(df, clusters == CT, select=c("obs_log2FD", "FDR", "boot_mean_log2FD", "boot_CI_2.5", "boot_CI_97.5"))
    df$comparison <- paste0(S1, "_vs_", S2)
    data <- rbind(data, df)
  }
  
  comparison.rename <- c("AD_5622_vs_AD_ctrl"="PL_vs_AD", 
                         "AD_ctrl_vs_WT_ctrl"="AD_vs_WT", 
                         "AD_5622_vs_WT_ctrl"="PL_vs_WT")
  
  data$comparison <- comparison.rename[data$comparison]
  
  data$comparison <- factor(data$comparison, levels=c("AD_vs_WT", 
                                                      "PL_vs_AD", 
                                                      "PL_vs_WT"),
                            ordered=T)
  
  data$point.color <- ifelse(abs(data$obs_log2FD) > log2(1.2) & data$FDR<0.05,
                             "|FC| > 1.2 & FDR < 0.05", "NS")
  
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(comparison, obs_log2FD)) +
    geom_linerange(aes(ymin=boot_CI_2.5, ymax=boot_CI_97.5)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=-log2(1.2), color="grey", linetype="dashed") +
    geom_hline(yintercept=log2(1.2), color="grey", linetype="dashed") +
    geom_point(aes(color=point.color)) + 
    labs(title="", y=Title) + 
    scale_color_manual(values=c("|FC| > 1.2 & FDR < 0.05"="red", "NS"="black")) + 
    theme_nature() + 
    theme(axis.title.x=element_blank(), 
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle=30, vjust = 0.7, hjust=.7),
          legend.position = "none",
          axis.title.y=element_markdown())

}
```


```{r}
# Plot separately:
# Proportion change relative to Micro.
CT <- "Micro_1"
Title <- "In Micro"
in.prefix <- "../002SC/004Seurat_out/003proportion/scProportionTest_subtype/Micro"

p1 <- permutationPlot.target_ct(CT, Title, in.prefix)

# Proportion change relative to Total cell.
CT <- "Micro_1"
Title <- "In Total Cells"
in.prefix <- "../002SC/004Seurat_out/003proportion/scProportionTest/anno.L3"

p2 <- permutationPlot.target_ct(CT, Title, in.prefix)

if (F) {
  ggsave("publish_new/fig5a.top.pdf", p1, width=1.4861, height=1.8333, device = cairo_pdf)
  ggsave("publish_new/fig5a.top.png", p1, width=1.4861, height=1.8333, dpi=300)
  ggsave("publish_new/fig5a.bottom.pdf", p2, width=1.4861, height=1.8333, device = cairo_pdf)
  ggsave("publish_new/fig5a.bottom.png", p2, width=1.4861, height=1.8333, dpi=300)
  # Issue: Low spatial efficiency.
}

if (F) {
  # Save space:
  # 1. y-axis only shows partial info: in total cell, in microglia. Common y-axis label: "DAM Proportion Change (Log2 Fold Change)"
  # 2. top doesn't show x-axis label.
  # 3. Merge using patchwork.
  p1 <- p1 + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank())
  pall <- p1 / p2

  ggsave("publish_new/fig5a.pdf", pall, width=1.3181, height=2.406, device = cairo_pdf)
  ggsave("publish_new/fig5a.png", pall, width=1.3181, height=2.406, dpi=300)
  # Issues:
  # 1. Inconsistent numeric precision on y-axis;
  # 2. Gap between top and bottom is too wide.
  # 3. Failed to highlight the key point: difference between total cell and Micro.
}

p1 <- p1 + ylim(c(-1, 6)) + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank())
p2 <- p2 + ylim(c(-1, 6)) + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank())

ggsave("publish_new/fig5a.top.pdf", p1, width=1.2616, height=1.205, device = cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish_new/fig5a.top.png", p1, width=1.2616, height=1.205, dpi=300)
ggsave("publish_new/fig5a.bottom.pdf", p2, width=1.2616, height=1.205, device = cairo_pdf)
ggsave("publish_new/fig5a.bottom.png", p2, width=1.2616, height=1.205, dpi=300)


# fig 5a, key point not highlighted enough, added a third version:
# Only show PL vs AD, side-by-side vs total cell and vs microglia.
p1.data <- p1@data
p1.data.sub <- subset(p1.data, comparison == "PL_vs_AD")
p1.data.sub$relative_to <- "in microglia"
p2.data <- p2@data
p2.data.sub <- subset(p2.data, comparison == "PL_vs_AD")
p2.data.sub$relative_to <- "in total cells"
p3.data <- rbind(p1.data.sub, p2.data.sub)

p3 <- ggplot(p3.data, aes(relative_to, obs_log2FD)) +
```
